nodes.label: 1: Global electricity generation trends and fuel mix (overall patterns, increases, relative shares)
2: Country comparisons and differences (China vs US vs India; population, development, per-capita, why differences)
3: Economic shocks and dips in electricity generation (2008 crisis, COVID/pandemic; recession-demand link)
4: Climate change, global warming, pollution, environmental and health harms from fossil fuels
5: Calls for transition to clean/renewable energy; solutions, advocacy, policy, regulation, future goals
6: Nuclear power advocacy and debate (easy fix, zero carbon, lack of capacity, environmentalists oppose, safety/reliability)
7: Questions about why fossil fuels remain dominant; affordability, accessibility, resource availability, industry influence
8: Graph reading, structure, units, methodology, and data interpretation questions (GWh, gigawatt, stacked area, sampling, calculations)
9: Local/community and personal relevance (impacts, lived experience, place-based notes, solar panels, factories, energy costs, EVs)
10: Energy demand growth drivers (population growth, electrification, technology integration, overpopulation/resource limits)
11: Social/political critique and responsibility narratives (profit motives, addiction to lifestyle, government fear, existential framing)
12: Headlines/titles proposed by commenters (including blank headline placeholders)
13: Meta moderation, instructions, shout-outs, and article logistics (NYT moderation posts, deadlines, reveal, next graph)
14: Personal introductions, greetings, and autobiographical asides (names, hello, junior high memory, sign-offs)
15: Solar panels/rooftop solar cost and household savings questions (scenario planning)
16: Miscellaneous/unclear or affective reactions not fitting other themes (sad estimate, 'crazy' gigawatts, generic intrigue)
